opp.name: URC_S2P 2026

March, 2024 18th Mar - No response from Lauro & Billy; Joyce has left the organization, she directed me to Ivan Alcatara who is the IT Solutions Lead in her place currently; Spoke to Ivan, said he was aware of the project on schedule, they were looking into other vendors as well, but was not aware of it in detail, asked to drop a mail so he will forward it to his Boss. 25% Confidence 13th May 2026- Discussion with Joannie (joannie.festejo@urc.com.ph) who is senior SChain VP- she will discuss with Vicky Yap (CPO) to set up meeting for me and AD to demo and see how we can bring AI into URC. 24th Jan - BD following with the POCs Billy & Lauro via calls, mails 8th Jan - ck to qualify- account going silent. opp.ais_why:
Zycus Opportunity Assessment
Parameter	Score
AI Adoption Maturity	8.5

contacts.name: Jathniel Ibañes
contacts.title: Global Process Owner - Source to Pay

items.area: No approved procurement roadmap / no formal evaluation
items.quote: There's no concrete plan so far, but there will be

items.area: No quantified business case (cost, savings, cycle-time baseline)
items.quote: the biggest priority... the ROI... this is not a cheap investment

items.area: Digital Transformation Office approvers not yet mapped or engaged
items.quote: the digital transformation office are the approvers

sources: SF ais_why (Zycus Opportunity Assessment, procurement complexity 9)
metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated qualitative pain but has quantified nothing. On the 20 May 2026 call the relevant stakeholder said the priority is 'the ROI on... because this is not a cheap investment', and framed the driver as strategy and category-scale improvement across multiple territories and plants rather than a cost figure: 'we have multiple territories... multiple procurement people deployed on each plant'. He was explicit that manual-to-digital alone is not the trigger. No cost-per-transaction baseline, headcount, cycle-time, or savings target exists, and Carl Kimball flagged the internal bar: 'when these things hit your CFO and your CEO, it's gotta be pretty solid business case.' Quantifying the value case with a no-cost savings/ROI baseline is the move that makes this real.

sources: Avoma call 20 May 2026 (Jath requested an AI use-case one-pager)
champion.narrative: No confirmed champion. Jathniel Ibanes (Global Process Owner - Source to Pay) is the most engaged working contact and is genuinely building the transformation roadmap, but the deal team flags him as too junior to drive the deal ('Jath too junior... we need to navigate to CPO level', Next Step 26 May 2026), and there is no CPO above him. Joannie Festejo (Director, Planning & Logistics) showed the earlier initiative, offering on 13 May 2026 to connect internally with Vicky Yap, but with no confirmed follow-through. Nobody has yet advocated for Zycus to the approvers or shared internal documents. The opening is to develop Jath with the AI use-case one-pager he requested while securing Vicky Yap directly at Horizon so advocacy sits at power level, not just at process-owner level.

sources: Avoma call 20 May 2026 (ERP-only today; Deloitte SI; internal assessment with a partner)
SF Next_Step__c 18 Mar 2024 (looking into other vendors, none named)
competition.narrative: There is no named software rival in play; the real competition is the status quo and the buyer's own ERP-first instinct. URC runs procurement today on SAP (moving to S/4 HANA with Deloitte as SI) plus a Mendix supplier portal, and any new platform must fit that ERP transformation. Jathniel confirmed a parallel transformation assessment is already running with an unnamed partner, and a March 2024 note recorded the buyer 'looking into other vendors as well' with none named. Zycus's wedge is the agentic-AI / Merlin story the buyer explicitly wants (ERP-native AI called 'very limited') and the conglomerate-transformation proof (SM Group, GCash, BDO in the Philippines). The risk is diffuse, no-forcing-event inertia rather than a competitor being preferred.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is procurement fragmentation across a multi-territory, multi-plant footprint with no unified source-to-pay platform. Jathniel Ibanes on 20 May 2026: 'we have multiple territories... multiple procurement people deployed on each plant', and today 'we only have our ERP as our procurement tool' plus a Mendix supplier portal. He confirmed appetite to unify the full S2P suite ('the goal is to have a new one just to be platform') and acknowledged ERP-native AI is 'very limited' so they want AI in the procurement platform. The pain is strategic and recognised but not yet urgent: it competes for priority with the ongoing S/4 HANA ERP rollout across countries. The pain owner is procurement leadership (Vicky Yap), who is only now engaging.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (NDA can be initiated to enable data sharing)
Avoma call 20 May 2026 (partner-led implementation, KPMG/regional SI)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are undefined and months away. The most recent paper signal is historical: the Next Step notes 'NDA can be initiated to enable Data sharing' as a future step that was never executed. No MSA, SOW, InfoSec review, procurement channel, or legal timeline has been discussed, and there is no incumbent contract-expiration forcing function (the buyer runs procurement through their own ERP). The buyer flagged a preference for face-to-face meetings and expects an external transformation partner (KPMG or a regional SI) to be involved in any implementation. Visibility will only come once a roadmap is approved and a formal evaluation begins.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Vicky Yap = Decision Maker, Head of Procurement
SF task 15 Jun 2026 (Vicky Yap Horizon reply)
Avoma call 20 May 2026 (Jathniel: 'we don't have a CPO... our VP is procurement'; Digital Transformation Office 'are the approvers')
economic_buyer.narrative: Vicky Yap (Head of Procurement) is marked Decision Maker in Salesforce contact roles and is the budget-relevant procurement leader; on the 20 May 2026 call Jathniel confirmed there is no CPO at URC or the parent JG Summit and that a VP of Procurement is the senior procurement authority. For the first time she is engaging directly: she replied to the Horizon invite on 15 Jun 2026 and agreed to attend with a three-person team, and was offered a panel spot. Approval, however, runs through the Digital Transformation Office, which Jathniel named as 'the approvers' who sign off technology investments, so budget authority is shared between procurement leadership and that office. The move is to convert the Horizon relationship into a direct procurement-leadership working session with Vicky and to map the Digital Transformation Office approvers.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 13 May 2026 (Joannie to connect internally with Vicky Yap)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer's process is still pre-decision and internally owned. Per Jathniel on 20 May 2026, individual departments 'propose or present the pain points and then propose the solutions', the Digital Transformation Office acts as the approvers, and any implementation would be overseen by an internal transformation power plus 'maybe an external partner'. The stated near-term milestone is buyer-internal: 'the goal is to have an approved road map for this year.' A separate transformation assessment is already underway with an unnamed partner ('we have already started an assessment... we have a specific partner on that'). Zycus has no formal step booked; the process advances only if Zycus helps shape the roadmap and gets in front of the approvers alongside Vicky Yap.

decision_criteria.narrative: There are no confirmed evaluation criteria because the buyer has not entered a formal evaluation. On the 20 May 2026 call Jathniel described an aspiration to unify sourcing, contracts, vendors and P2P onto 'a new one just to be platform' and to take 'a holistic approach... not just the digital', and stressed the initiative must fit the wider ERP/S4 HANA transformation being run with the relevant stakeholder also signalled AI as a differentiator ('we understand is very limited... we're really looking forward to have still in our digital platform'). These are directional wants, not scored criteria, and no RFI/RFP or requirement document exists. Running a value/discovery workshop to co-author the criteria before the roadmap is approved is the move.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — No competitor is preferred or ahead; the buyer is not leaning to any vendor. The single strongest CURRENT threat is the status quo / do-nothing inertia: URC runs procurement through its ERP today, has no approved roadmap, and faces internal politics across the JG Summit conglomerate, so the deal can drift indefinitely without a forcing event. ERP-native procurement (SAP S/4 HANA, implemented with Deloitte) is the incumbent alternative the platform must displace. An internal transformation assessment with an unnamed partner is running in parallel. Historic (Mar 2024) 'other vendors' were never named and are dormant. Zycus's wedge is the agentic-AI/Merlin story the buyer explicitly wants and conglomerate-transformation proof points (SM Group, GCash, BDO).

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: No competitor is preferred or ahead; the buyer is not leaning to any vendor.

bullets.text: No CPO exists; the Head of Procurement (Vicky Yap) is only just engaging and approval runs through a Digital Transformation Office that Zycus has never contacted.
No approved roadmap and no formal evaluation; the 30 Oct 2026 close date is not credible against a buyer still targeting roadmap approval this year.
$250k is a placeholder with no quantified ROI/savings case, while the buyer named a solid CFO/CEO business case as the bar for approval.
JG Summit conglomerate politics and subsidiary autonomy slow any group-level procurement mandate ('a lot of pushback... some wanna do this, some wanna do that').

items: Book a face-to-face procurement-leadership session with Vicky Yap at Horizon SEA, 21-22 Jul. (by 10 Jul)
Deliver Jath the AI use-case one-pager plus Philippines proof points (SM Group, GCash). (by 15 Jul)
Open a thread to Rebbeca Yap (VP Procurement) to reach senior procurement authority. (by 30 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Universal Robina Corporation — Qualified · $250K · closes 30 Oct

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 13 is carried by momentum 61 vs stage-expected 50; exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 61 (moving forward): seniority rising (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (13), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 61, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 8.0 Moderate matches the call: Jathniel actively wants AI in the procurement platform and acknowledged ERP-native AI is limited, and IT is already experimenting with agentic use cases. Appetite is real but exploratory, not yet tied to a scored requirement, so the field reads slightly conservative versus the buyer's stated interest.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain (multi-territory fragmentation, ERP-only tooling) and a stated ROI priority, but no numbers: 'the biggest priority... the ROI... this is not a cheap investment' (Jathniel, 20 May 2026).

items.change: Jathniel Ibanes registered for Horizon SEA 2026; Zycus covered the full event package for Vicky Yap, Luke and Jath
Vicky Yap (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker) replied she is interested in Horizon with 3 attendees - first DM-level engagement on record
Discovery call with Jathniel Ibanes: buyer confirmed no approved procurement roadmap yet, S/4 HANA transformation underway with Deloitte, ERP-only procurement today
Stage set to Qualified and close date rolled from 2024 to 30 Oct 2026

deal_movement.summary: After ~32 months mostly at mid/junior level, engagement has just risen to the Decision Maker: Vicky Yap (Head of Procurement) accepted the Horizon SEA 2026 invite in Jun 2026 with a three-person team. The 20 May 2026 discovery call reset the picture to genuinely early (no approved roadmap, ERP-only today). The verdict is strengthening versus the prior Off Track read, but the deal is still pre-evaluation.

items.risk: Budget-relevant procurement leader and closest thing to an economic buyer (no CPO exists); engaged for the first time via Horizon but not yet in a deal conversation. Losing this newly-opened thread would stall the deal.

items.detail: No CPO exists; the Head of Procurement (Vicky Yap) is only just engaging and approval runs through a Digital Transformation Office that Zycus has never contacted.
No approved roadmap and no formal evaluation; the 30 Oct 2026 close date is not credible against a buyer still targeting roadmap approval this year.
$250k is a placeholder with no quantified ROI/savings case, while the buyer named a solid CFO/CEO business case as the bar for approval.
JG Summit conglomerate politics and subsidiary autonomy slow any group-level procurement mandate ('a lot of pushback... some wanna do this, some wanna do that').

critical_signals.text: Vicky Yap, the Head of Procurement, engaged for the first time in over two years and agreed to bring three people to the July procurement event, but we have not yet met her in a deal conversation.
The 20 May discovery reset expectations to early: URC has no approved procurement roadmap and runs procurement only through its ERP today.
The buyer named ROI as the biggest priority and flagged a solid CFO and CEO business case as the approval bar, yet no savings or value case has been built.
No vendor is preferred; the real threat is do-nothing inertia and ERP-native procurement, so a forcing event is needed rather than a competitive displacement.
The buyer asked for a partner recommendation covering holistic transformation, not just digital, signalling implementation and SI fit will shape the evaluation.

Joannie Festejo showed early willingness to escalate to Vicky Yap without confirmed follow-through. What has developed: a genuine working contact (Jath) and, newly, direct interest from the Decision Maker (Vicky) via Horizon. What is missing: a power-level advocate who will carry Zycus to the Digital Transformation Office approvers.

items.expected_effect: Converts event attendance into a working deal conversation with the Decision Maker and opens the path to power beyond junior Jath.

items.action: Deliver Jath the AI use-case one-pager plus Philippines proof points (SM Group, GCash).
items.trigger: Akshay committed to a one-pager and Jath asked to socialise it internally
items.expected_effect: Arms the working contact to advocate internally and keeps the Merlin/agentic-AI wedge in front of the roadmap team.

items.trigger: No CPO exists; a VP of Procurement is the senior authority and has no engagement on record
items.expected_effect: Multi-threads to power and reduces reliance on a single junior contact.

items.expected_effect: Creates a forcing event, quantifies value, and positions Zycus inside the roadmap the buyer is approving this year.

items: Vicky Yap (Decision Maker) engaged for the first time in 32 months (15 Jun 2026)
Buyer is exploratory: no approved roadmap, no RFP, ERP-only today
Recent touches are rep-initiated event invites; buyer has not requested a deal working session
confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and, importantly, rising to Decision-Maker level via Horizon, but momentum toward an actual purchase decision is thin and event-driven rather than process-driven.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 13 Oct 2023, ~32 months in cycle; Qualified since 13 May 2026 with no submission-date series recorded (no Formal Eval / RFP / shortlist). Close date 30 Oct 2026 is ~119 days out. On the 20 May 2026 discovery call the buyer stated there is no fixed roadmap yet and the goal is only to get a roadmap approved this year, so a realistic enterprise S2P cycle (12-18 months from roadmap approval) puts a genuine close well beyond Oct 2026. Required pace to hold the date (RFP in 30 days, shortlist by Sep, commercials by Oct) is not supported by any buyer commitment; the live action is converting the new Vicky Yap engagement at Horizon into a working procurement-leadership session.
evidence: Vicky Yap (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker) replied to the Horizon invite on 15 Jun 2026: 'I'm interested in the conference... I'm looking at 3 attendees' - her first recorded engagement in 32 months
Zycus confirmed and covered the full Horizon SEA 2026 package for Vicky, Luke and Jath (task 18 Jun 2026), and offered Vicky a procurement-leader panel spot (task 16 Jun 2026)
Discovery call 20 May 2026 (Jathniel Ibanes): 'There's no concrete plan so far, but there will be' and 'the goal is to have an approved road map for this year'
Buyer works procurement today only through ERP: 'currently we only have our ERP as our procurement tool' plus a Mendix supplier portal (Jathniel, 20 May 2026)
Stage still Qualified / ForecastCategory Pipeline; no RFP, shortlist, demo, or commercials on record
$250k is a placeholder for the full suite with no ROI case built and no economic-buyer sign-off
Next Step (26 May 2026): '50% confidence... we need to navigate to CPO level, Jath too junior'
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal is alive and, for the first time in 32 months, the Head of Procurement (Vicky Yap) is engaging: she agreed to bring three people to Horizon SEA in July 2026. But the buyer has no approved procurement roadmap, no RFP, no quantified business case, and works today only through their ERP, so the 30 Oct 2026 close date is not credible and momentum is still thin.

flags: Multi-thread up: today's only working thread is Jath (too junior); use Horizon to convert Vicky Yap and reach Rebbeca Yap (VP Procurement) at power level.
Manufacture the business case: turn the buyer's stated ROI priority and AIS 8 AI appetite into a no-cost savings baseline the roadmap and CFO can defend.
Sell the conglomerate proof: anchor URC on the SM Group / GCash / BDO Philippines wins to break JG Summit inertia and position a group-wide S2P standard.
best_practice_check.summary: The gate is power access and a business case, not activity. Three levers raise win probability now.

customer_preference.evidence: Jathniel was neutral and exploratory on 20 May 2026 ('explore the current platforms that we have or if there's any platform implemented locally'); no Zycus-specific lean, no proposal or CPO-demo request from the buyer.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is preferred or ahead; the buyer is not leaning to any vendor. The single strongest CURRENT threat is the status quo / do-nothing inertia: URC runs procurement through its ERP today, has no approved roadmap, and faces internal politics across the JG Summit conglomerate, so the deal can drift indefinitely without a forcing event. ERP-native procurement (SAP S/4 HANA, implemented with Deloitte) is the incumbent alternative the platform must displace. An internal transformation assessment with an unnamed partner is running in parallel. Historic (Mar 2024) 'other vendors' were never named and are dormant. Zycus's wedge is the agentic-AI/Merlin story the buyer explicitly wants and conglomerate-transformation proof points (SM Group, GCash, BDO).

competitors.name: Status Quo / Do Nothing
competitors.quote: There's no concrete plan so far, but there will be... the goal is to have an approved road map for this year
competitors.how_we_win: Create a forcing event: co-build the ROI/savings baseline that the roadmap needs so inaction has a visible cost, and anchor it to the CFO/CEO business-case bar the buyer named.

competitors.name: ERP-native procurement (SAP S/4 HANA)
competitors.quote: currently we only have our ERP as our procurement tool... we understand is very limited
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with best-of-breed S2P + agentic Merlin where ERP-native AI is admittedly limited; position as the procurement layer on top of S/4 HANA, not a rip-and-replace.

competitors.name: Internal transformation assessment (unnamed partner)
competitors.quote: we have already started an assessment, but that's from... we have a specific partner on that
competitors.how_we_win: Insert Zycus into the roadmap work now so the assessment recommends a best-of-breed S2P platform rather than defaulting to ERP-only.

competitors.name: Unknown vendors under evaluation (unconfirmed)
competitors.quote: they were looking into other vendors as well, but was not aware of it in detail
competitors.how_we_win: No named rival; keep discovery open to surface any shortlist before an RFP forms.

items.deliverable: Confirm a procurement-leadership working session (Vicky Yap and team) beyond the Horizon event
items.grounding_quote: Potential follow-up session to be arranged with procurement leadership. Preference for future meetings to be conducted face-to-face

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS 8.0 (Moderate) with strong sub-scores on procurement complexity (9), supplier ecosystem scale (9) and enterprise transformation momentum (9). The buyer explicitly wants applied agentic AI and finds ERP-native AI 'very limited', so the Merlin / agentic story is a live wedge, but it has not yet been demonstrated to the buyer. Under-positioned only in that no Merlin demo or AI proof has been put in front of URC yet.

items.quote: the goal is to have a new one just to be platform
items.criterion: Unified full-suite S2P platform (not point tools)

items.quote: we understand is very limited... really looking forward to have still in our digital platform
items.criterion: Real, applied agentic AI beyond ERP-native

items.quote: the biggest priority... the ROI... because this is not a cheap investment
items.criterion: Solid ROI / business case for CFO and CEO

items.quote: we tend to lean a little heavier with the ones who are strong with SAP
items.criterion: Fit with S/4 HANA transformation and strong SAP-integration partner

customer_expectations_fit.summary: URC wants a single, holistic source-to-pay platform with real agentic AI that fits its S/4 HANA transformation and delivers a defensible ROI case, and expects strong SAP-integration partner support. Zycus aligns on the platform and AI story; the exposure is the missing quantified value case and an unapproved internal roadmap.
